source.definition: to emit or reflect light brightly; to appear bright or radiant

explanation.sourceText: The English verb "shine" originates from Old English "scinan", meaning "to emit light or glow". It is related to other Germanic words conveying brightness and light.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „shine” provine din engleza veche „scinan”, care înseamnă „a emite lumină sau a străluci”. Este înrudit cu alte cuvinte germanice care exprimă luminozitate și lumină.

source.additional: A regular verb. Commonly used to describe light reflections or radiant surfaces.

target.definition: a avea o suprafață strălucitoare sau a apărea strălucitor

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb „a străluci” is an internal derivation formed from „strălucă”, which has Slavic roots but the current form is specific to Romanian.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a străluci” este format intern, derivat din „strălucă”, cu rădăcini slave („străluciti”), dar forma actuală este specifică limbii române.

target.additional: Regular verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). Can also be used in poetic or literary contexts.

examples.sourceText: We shine together in the spotlight.
examples.targetText: Noi strălucim împreună în lumina refletoarelor.
Ei strălucesc în cadrul prezentării lor.

examples.sourceText: I polished the car; it shines now.
examples.targetText: Am lustruit mașina, ea strălucește acum.

examples.sourceText: The jewelry shone in the light.
I will shine when I perform.
examples.targetText: Voi străluci când mă voi prezenta.

examples.sourceText: I would shine if I had the chance.
examples.targetText: Aș străluci dacă aș avea ocazia.

examples.sourceText: Shine in the eyes of others!

learningTips.text: Remember "shine" by imagining a bright star shining in the night sky, just like the Romanian verb "a străluci" means to glow or sparkle.
Think of "strălucit" as an adjective describing something brilliantly shining, similar to "shiny" in English.
Connect "a străluci" with polished jewelry or a shiny car to keep the meaning vivid in your mind.
The English word "shine" and Romanian "a străluci" both relate to light and brightness, making them easy to associate.
